pattern: Sort meetings by start day, merge overlapping intervals, then subtract the total covered days from the available days.
hints: Sort the meetings by their start day so you can merge overlapping or adjacent ones in a single pass.
After merging, each merged interval [s,e] covers e-s+1 days; subtract all those from the total.
Be careful with overlapping meetings: when extending the current interval, take the maximum of the two end days.

edge_inputs.description: single meeting of one day, lots of free days
single meeting of one day at start
meeting spans from day 1 to days with no free days
many adjacent meetings that together cover everything

stress.description: many random meetings on a large day range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    total_days = 1000000
    n = min(size, 500)
    meetings = []
    for _ in range(n):
        s = rng.randint(1, total_days)
        e = rng.randint(s, min(s + 100, total_days))
        meetings.append([s, e])
    return [total_days, meetings]
